routes.name: 20 Feet to France
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start under the fixed draw four metres left of Zack Attack. Engaging opening moves lead to a good stance below the roof.Fromhere,powerfulandgymnasticmovesleadtoa jug at the lip (a somewhat hidden sidepull undercling in the roof helps). Gain the headwall and enjoy the easy pocket climbing to the anchors.

routes.name: Blondie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nice movement through pockets and blocks leads to a thin headwall with nice technical climbing on crimps.
routes.Location: 2nd route left of the big corner

routes.name: Boneshaker
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A nice technical line with varied movement.

routes.name: Cathy's Challenge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Stay right of the second and third last bolts at the top of the climb.
routes.Location: 1 metre right of a small roundish cedar located five metres up the cliff face.

routes.name: The Deuce
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start near plate 88 and climb up right to the first bolt.Climb the intimidating bulges to the anchors.

routes.name: Forgotten Rebels
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the short roof to the right of boneshaker

routes.name: Jen's Corner
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Tricky crux moving onto right facing wall.
routes.Location: 10m left of plate 88.
routes.Protection: 8 bolts, use longer draws for bottom 4 to avoid rope drag over top ledge

routes.name: Journey to Ixtlan
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy and enjoyable movement on section up until the roof, then tough roof crux and sustained steep climbing to the top.

routes.name: Left Popsicle Twin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A short route that would be a nice introductory climb for a beginner or new leader. A lot of big jugs and pockets with a short crux about mid-way up.
routes.Location: First route to the right of the corner. See beta photo.
routes.Protection: Four bolts to two-bolt anchor with rings.

routes.name: Mad Dog
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ledgy climbing directly to the left of the huge flake

routes.name: Nancy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first route on the cliff, located seven metres right of plate 88. Typical of many of the routes on this wall, pull on large flakes, ledges and pockets to the anchors.

routes.name: Right Popsicle Twin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun pocket climbing to a bolted anchor.
routes.Location: Climbers right of Swiss Cheese, across on the corner wall, second route from the left.
routes.Protection: Well protected with bolts to a bolted anchor with rings to lower.

routes.name: Rude Roof (Closed Project)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This line requires extensive cleaning and the reposi- tioning of some bolts. Please stay off of it until this work is completed.

routes.name: Sick Runouts
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Scramble up the first 5m to enjoyable face and corner climbing above.
routes.Protection: Closely bolted (definitely not runout!), but the route can also be done entirely on trad.

routes.name: Sid
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Reachy moves on good holds. One of the best routes at The Turtle.
routes.Location: The second route you come across, just left of Nancy.

routes.name: The Snapper
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the face to the right of the arete. Short crux going around left side of the roof (undercling?). Significantly Easier climbing to the top. Short but fun route.
routes.Location: Near the far left end of the crag. Look for a sign that reads, "THIS IS THE END OF THE CRAG. Please go no further, at the request of the landowner" and features a very cute cartoon turtle

routes.name: The Swan King
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sustained climbing on lots of flakes and good holds. Two roofs to pull near the top make it tough for the grade.

routes.name: Swiss Cheese
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy climbing on nice pockets
routes.Location: First route left of the big corner

routes.name: Target, Guam
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Double roofs and a traverse, happy climbing!
routes.Location: Third to last bolted line from the left side of the crag.

routes.name: Vuvuzela
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the steep roof above number 89

routes.name: We Were Promised Jetpacks
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb onto the block and work your way up the thin crack to the anchors.
routes.Location: Right of the large detached block, 12 metres left of the roof.

routes.name: Zack Attack
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb out on the right side of the roof then face climb the pockets to a bolted anchor with rings.
routes.Location: Right side of the 'great roof' area, 5m left of "We were promised jetpacks."
routes.Protection: Bolts to the anchor, rings to lower off.

routes.name: Zen and the Art of Archery
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoyable climbing to a tricky crux sequence.

routes.name: Bookends
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Classic stemming with a low crux
routes.Location: Obvious open book.

routes.name: Thesaureor
routes.FA:: Randy Kielbasiewicz, Kirsti Allen. Equipped David Smart (2017)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Blank face with horizontal cracks and ledges
routes.Location: Just left of Bookends corner

routes.name: Big Game Tag
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The large offwidth through the center of the ground zero ledge.

routes.name: Burrito
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slab somewhat technical face start with an interesting overhanging finish.
routes.Location: Located on the right face of a dihedral towards the left side of the ledge.

routes.name: Missing Toe
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Initially rated 5.10b, this route’s name nods to a mishap involving a lawn mower and one of Ontario’s key route developers, Chris Oates (don’t worry, he’s fine). Enjoy excellent face climbing on sloping holds, but be careful as you approach the anchors!
routes.Location: On it own, by the descent gully...
routes.Protection: Bolts (I forgot how many but it not long so take a look you will like be ok) and 2 bolt with rings at anchors

routes.name: Nacho
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Techy face and slab climbing, somewhat awkward movement.
routes.Location: Shares the same start as Enchilada, the first route after scrambling up the small 5 foot pinnacle chimney on the ledge.

routes.name: Nova
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Slab like start leads to hand/off width crack up top.
routes.Location: Route starts just right of the blocky chimney on the big game ledge.

routes.name: Sundog
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Short 12 foot scramble to the first set of bolts, this is the left line that trends towards the wrap rings on the big game ledge.
routes.Location: Located climbers right of the big game ledge. It is the first bolted line accessible from the ground, though a short scramble is required.

routes.name: Taco
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The route follows a chossy start over a roof and into higher quality slab finish.
routes.Location: Farthest left route on Big game ledge, nearly shares a line with Hybrid.

routes.name: Tortilla
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Perhaps the most five ten fun on the ledge. Two small cruxes separated by a great rest.
routes.Location: Located on the right face of the arête before the chossy roof on the furthest left side of the ledge.

routes.name: The Black Wall
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep climbing with a very high first bolt.
routes.Location: Just left of the corner where 'Easy Steps' begins.

routes.name: Easy Does It
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The short corner next to Siren

routes.name: Easy Steps
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: From Gooseberry gully, walk south.  You come to a prominent, left facing corner.  This is the location of Easy Steps.  The route goes up the obvious line of weakness into the corner.
routes.Protection: Excellent protection and is a great route for the learning trad climber.

routes.name: Fixing the Bridge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fixing the Bridge

routes.name: Good to the Last Drop
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Retro bolted.

routes.name: Half of 64
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: From Gooseberry Gully, walk south. Pass Too Fast in the Fog. Continue until you see the '32,' which is painted in yellow at the base of the climb. The route has a blocky start that continues up to a left facing corner. Stem the left facing corner and step left to the wrap station. This is a very accessible 5.8, with only one crux move, which is a well protected stem.

routes.name: No Sweat
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Solid blocky corner.
routes.Location: Paint #36

routes.name: Rimes With Orange
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb dark corner system. Climb out the small roof using dual crack systems.
routes.Location: 10m left of Gooseberry Gully

routes.name: Sharptooth
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Rest on a sharp hold at the roof before attempting the crux.  After some face climbing, a second roof guards the anchors.

routes.name: Too Fast in Fog
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mt. Nemo hosts a number of easy to moderate, trad climbs. This route is no exception and is a great route for the introductory trad climber.  The route is technically straight forward and easy to protect.  From GooseBerry Gully, turn right and walk south.  You will almost immediately come to a prominent. left facing corner.  The route ascends the middle of the south facing wall.
routes.Protection: Pro = G.  There are bolts at the top, but the hangers are missing.  To descend, rappel from any tree.  Or alternatively, walk north to GooseBerry Gully and walk back.

routes.name: Circling the Areola
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route ascends the wall immediately to the left of Tits Up.  This is a good TR after doing Tits.  Just place a directional.  The crux is a tease and hence explains the route's name.

routes.name: Friday Fliers
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: From Gooseberry Gully, hike north. Pass The Dead Zone and enter the Big Section. Just north of Tits up is a prominent, left facing corner. A very faded, yellow '16d' marks the route. This corner has very loose rock at its base. Traverse in from the left to stay safe. Above the loose junk is very clean and enjoyable climbing, including a wide crack that continues up the face to a roof. Traverse around the roof to clean finger locks and the wrap station at the guano ledge.

routes.name: Hollow Winds
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes: No text - use the 'Improve This Page' link to add something useful!

routes.name: Mike's Peel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is located roughly in the middle of the Big Section.  It is identified by a yellow '20' at its base.  The route starts by ascending a crispy face, aiming at 1 O'clock to a left facing corner.  The left facing corner continues to a large ledge, which is not the most obvious from the ground (good rest stance).  Finish by pulling the peel move to the left and zag back right to the tree and cliff top.
routes.Protection: Trad.  The start of the route passes through questionable rock quality, but there is ample protection.  The quality of rock from the left facing corner to the cliff top is superb, with many quality moves.

routes.name: Nips
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route ascends the wall immediately to the right of Tits Up.  This is a good TR after doing Tits.  Just place a directional below and to the right of the tree in a solid crack.  The route is easy for its grade with only one 5.7 move.

routes.name: Now What
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is very accessible trad route that is superb for the introductory trad climber.  The route hosts two variations that are technically straight forward and easy to protect.  From GooseBerry Gully walk north.  Pass The Dead Zone area and enter The Big Section.  You will make a slight turn left.  The numbers 19A and 19B will be painted in yellow on the rock.  These markings simply denote different starts to the route.  Both variations are 5.7 and converge at a roof, of which a crack bisects.  Pull the roof and traverse right at 1 O'clock to the wrap station.
routes.Protection: Protection: G.  There exists a sling anchor at the top to rappel from.

routes.name: Tits Up!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is yet another, classic Mt. Nemo technically straight forward, accessible and easy to protect route for the introductory trad climber.  Heck, everyone should climb it.  It is just plain fun.  From GooseBerry Gully walk north.  Pass The Dead Zone.  You will come to a left facing corner that rises approximately 4 meters.  Atop the left facing corner is a crack the ascends the majority of the face.  A small overhang sits atop the crack.  The route is marked with a yellow '16D.'  The original name of the route is unknown and so we arbitrarily gave it its present name.
routes.Protection: Protection: G.  Just when it starts feeling difficult, it will start getting easier.

routes.name: Duff
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Chossy near the bottom. Rock quality increases the higher you go
routes.Location: 5 metres right of the crack Dufte
routes.Protection: bolts. Only a single bolt anchor at the top of the route. Potentially a biner on the bolt below for redundancy, although this can easily be removed

routes.name: Dufte
routes.FA:: FA: Helmut Microys, Al Zob (1960-1965), FFA: Rick Clarke (late 1970's)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the obvious corner, past the tree. Good use of jamming

routes.name: Adrenaline Junkie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start up Schmaltzy Corner then take the direct bolted line through the roof. A few pieces are needed for the start.

routes.name: Dick's Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the obvious crack to the bulge. Surmount the roof and continue up the face.

routes.name: Hand that Cradles the Rock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb Helmut's route to start, then step around the arete to the bolt line. Alternatively can be accessed via Schmaltzy corner.
routes.Location: Immediately right of Helmut's route.

routes.name: Helmut's Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hike to Helmut's Wall.  You will come to a prominent buttress. The middle of the buttress has an overhand. The left side of this buttress has a left facing corner. The base of this corner has a yellow painted '6.' This is Helmut's. The route itself goes over solid rock. The climber must make a traverse right to get to the anchors. This is a good quality trad route.
routes.Protection: Protection: G.  One must traverse right to gain the wrap anchors.

routes.name: Are You A Fireman? So Why The Big Hose?
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First bolted route left of the descent gully and jagged arete housing Nicorette. Fourth clip is a permadraw to make cleaning easier.

routes.name: Cinderella
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The blocky offwidth leading into a chimney just left of Mean Streaks. Surmount the overhang using the left ledge combined with typical shimmy moves.
routes.Location: Paint #1

routes.name: Enchiridion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: At the start you will face thin crimps to difficult pockets. Manage your nerves for a difficult second clip. This is followed by easier climbing and then a second boulder crux to gain face.

routes.name: Farewell Infidel
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Three bolts on low-quality stone gets you to a compact ledge below a short roof bulge. Clear this and continue to the anchors behind a cedar branch. Less powerful climbers will appreciate a kneebar opportunity.

routes.name: Mean Streaks
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Hidden holds on sustained overhang, which can make this route difficult to clean.
routes.Location: Just right of the Cinderella crack, through the dark streaks in the rock.

routes.name: Nicorette
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Anchors are hard to spot from the ground. There is a one-move wonder mantle crux between bolts 2 and 3. Mildly pumpy otherwise.
routes.Location: Right of the Central Gully, on an arete.

routes.name: Polly Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts up a easy parallel cracks around a large pillar.   From the stance below the overhang, head right, taking the most direct line.  The roof appears difficult...commit and ye shall be rewarded with glory jugs!  Finish at a 2 bolt anchor.
routes.Location: Polly Direct is located about 20 meters left of the Central Gully, and 15 meters right of Rescue Plaque #40.  There is a painted #2 at the base.
routes.Protection: Gear to 4".  Unless you are comfortable running it out, I wouldn't leave the ground without a #4 C4 or equivalent.  Bolt anchors at the top.

routes.name: Polly Vicini
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb two cracks around the wedged pillar at the start, work your way through the shallow corner, and head left to join The Roof. Fun and one of the better trad options at Nemo.
routes.Location: Paint #2

routes.name: The Roof
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at the large chimney to the right of Ten Pound Lunker Bass. Climb up as it narrows heading to the top. A good Nemo offwidth practice option.
routes.Location: Paint #1

routes.name: Scarface
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Chossy start including a suspect flake at the first clip. Difficult crack climbing leads up to a roof.

routes.name: Ten Pound Lunker Bass
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Just left of the chimney at the start of Central Wall. Climbs up to bulge and then steps out right to a ledge before returning to the main face. Going through the bulge directly makes it 12-.

routes.name: Weak and Fat Will Work for Asparagus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Laybacks and good rests on this route. Bring your gadget arms for a reachy crux. Smooth sailing after that.

routes.name: Anticlimax
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Step up to the chimney and follow the path of least resistance to the top.
routes.Location: Paint #6B

routes.name: As You Like It
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fun! Climb the crack or left facing flake (easier). Climbing the flake requires a traverse to the left. Tricky part around the bottom, plus near the top with crimps. Generally nice jugs and big holds just when you need them though!
routes.Protection: The middle runout was recently removed with a new bolt.8 Bolts.

routes.name: Crazy Legs Ale
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Descend Alfred's Gully. Turn left.  You have arrived.  The route follows a prominent line of weakness up the wall.  To the left of this line of weakness is a bolted line, which is called Lost in the Forest (5.10-ish). Ascend the line of weakness.  Finish by traversing one move left to the anchors.
routes.Protection: Protection: G.  This is a very easy to protect route and is ideal for the introductory trad climber.

routes.name: Eric's First
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: After descending Alfred's Gully, walk right past the main Alfred's wall.  Look for a striking crack line.  The route has blocky features down low.  Up high is an incipient crack that arches up and right past a tree.  The route is marked with a yellow '6.'
routes.Protection: Protection: G. Small pro is helpful up high. Top anchors

routes.name: Leah
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy climbing to the first two bolts, then transitions to face climbing with thin crimps and a mono.  Move up an arete and then pull the roof (crux), then climbing eases up to anchors.
routes.Location: Right-most bolted line on Alfred's wall

routes.name: Live Bait
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start is polished.
routes.Location: Start below a cedar tree growing from the ledge.

routes.name: Lost in the Forest
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoyable climbing up the face provides many options.  Staying true to the bolt line will up the difficulty, but there is chalk to the left and the right in many places.  Choose your own adventure!
routes.Location: Start below a dead cedar tree growing from the ledge directly above Rescue Plaque #37.

routes.name: Running Towards Nothing
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easier ways up this route are available if you don't mind straying far from the bolts.
routes.Location: On the edge of the arete.

routes.name: The Seventh Origin of Alfred
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start by climbing up a nice crack/flake system.  Then make your way up on continuously difficult climbing through a bulge and onto the blank face above.  Navigate thin crimps through the technical crux, then fight the pump to the chains.  Excellent route!!!
routes.Location: Located 30 meters left of Alfred's Gully at Paint #10.

routes.name: Stand and Deliver
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Consistent difficulty the whole way through, without a distinct crux. Includes varied climbing: traversing, mantels, and crimps.

routes.name: Vanna, Pick Me a Number!
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Descend Alfred Gully.  as you come to the gully's opening at the base of the cliff, but before you down climb the little boulder section, look to your right.  You will see a bolted route.  This is Wide Load.  To the right of Wide Load is a crack and continuing line of weakness.  This is Vanna.  Ascend the route.  Just short of the cliff top, you can safely traverse left to the anchor station atop Wide load and rappel from there. This is a very easy 5.7 with only one 5.7 move.
routes.Protection: Trad.  If traversing to the Wide Load anchor, be sure to use a long sling as a directional.

routes.name: Wide Load
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Polished and often wet.
routes.Location: First sport route at the base of Alfred Gully.

routes.name: Wishbone
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overhanging sport route. Traverse left after the 5th bolt to join Live Bait.

routes.name: Big Bolts for Daddy
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoy cruising a lovely flake at the start before launching into more technical slab/face climbing crux. A jug haul to the top is your reward for crushing the technical crux.
routes.Location: Bolted line located furthest climbers right on the wall, located near a dirty embankment.

routes.name: Big O Tree
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crank through an enjoyable layback section, pull the overhang (crux), and then continue through easier but pumpy terrain to the finish.
routes.Location: Just right of Ten D Nitus

routes.name: Catch as Catch Can
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Tall Order. Head up to the dead tree, then use the dead tree and crack to get to the anchors. Loose blocks around bolts 3-4.
routes.Location: Bolted line to the right of Guido, near a large dead tree in the wall.

routes.name: Earthmen
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: To the right of Ten D Nitus is a nice crack that looks like it has decent gear. Ascend using a combination of jams, laybacks, and some face climbing.
routes.Location: The main crack to the right of Ten D Nitus. See beta photo.
routes.Protection: Gear to four inches. Can end on the anchor for Ten D Nitus.

routes.name: Fear and Loathing
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb through a section of crack and dihedral climbing to where Fear and Loathing and Train in Vain split, and veer left to the ledge under the left branch of bolts. Thin face climbing, good rests and many, many liebacks.

routes.name: Forever Young
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A fun, reachy climb over roofs with good rests in between. High first bolt.
routes.Location: Begin in the tall rectangular alcove right of Train in Vain, about 30 feet left of I Owe a Lot of Money to a Man Named Guido

routes.name: The Hunchback
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up chunky terrain to a dark overhanging arete where a solution pocket awaits you. Clear your headspace and pull through to easier terrain above.  Believe it or not, the overhang direct is about the same difficulty as if you climb the face.  Try to avoid going into the corner and using the tree.  Use a long draw below the arete.

routes.name: I Owe a Lot of Money to a Man Name Guido
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Fantastic route with fun technical moves. Start on an easy crack for about 10m before climbing to small edges and crimps on the face.

routes.name: Iguana Corner
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The dark blocky corner/chimney on the left end of the Iguana wall. Deposita one on the same ledge as the Alfred's Cave descent.
routes.Location: Far left end of Iguana wall

routes.name: Is She Pretty
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A popular climb as it is one of the easier bolted line at the wall. Climb through bulges and small roofs.
routes.Location: Start left of Third Time Lucky (5.8 TR)

routes.name: Nostalgia
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Nostalgia 5.10d

routes.name: The Odyssey
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: More or less retrobolted trad route "Axolotl" - Climb past a tree you’ll have no choice but to get intimate with to a burly haul fest to the finish.

routes.name: Revival
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoyable climbing with a mid crux through crimpy dimples and then memorable moves through the roof.
routes.Location: Bolted route between Hunchback and Upper Canada Rebellion.

routes.name: Tall Order
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Catch As Catch Can. Follow a crimpy line left of the bolts when you reach a dead tree, while avoiding the crack.
routes.Location: Bolted line to the right of Guido, near a large dead tree in the wall.

routes.name: Ten D Nitus
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoyable climbing leads to the crux surmounting the roof.
routes.Location: The bolted route just left of the corner crack (Earthmen).

routes.name: Third Time Lucky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is immediately to the right of Is She Pretty, which is an overhanging buttress at the left end of the area.  Ascend the finger crack in the book fold up to the roof.  Pull the roof (flexibility is a plus here) and ascend the left facing corner.  Traverse left to the anchors, which this route shares with Is She pretty.  A spectacular and sustained route.

routes.name: Train in Vain
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Sketchy bolts. Climb through a section of crack and dihedral climbing to where Fear and Loathing and Train in Vain split, and veer towards the crack on the right, in a run out area with black rock and a small tree. Surmount the roof using the wide crack and face holds, then try to control the pump on fairly sustained 5.11 climbing to the top.

routes.name: Upper Canada Rebellion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoy a nice cruise up some interest 5.10 climbing before kicking back and taking an amazing rest on a ledge before the roof. A short and slightly powerful sequence will see you through the roof and onto the anchors.
routes.Location: The route is located between two trees featuring a large blocky slab start. A red plaque with "34" (I think) is located under the first bolt.

routes.name: Big Game Tag
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Offwidth/squeeze chimney that takes protection.

routes.name: Burrito
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb short face to ledge.

routes.name: Eclipse
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Route to the right of Sundog.

routes.name: Enchilada
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Crux though a bulge requiring good footwork.

routes.name: Kung Fu Queue
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy face taking you to the roof. 3m left of Big Time Tag.

routes.name: Lost and Found
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Route to the right of Eclipse.
routes.Location: The route is 25m left of Castle Chimney.

routes.name: Nacho
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the short face to a ledge.

routes.name: Plastic Warriors Need Not Apply
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the fist route you come to just south of Falling Tom Gully.  Ascend the wall with the thin, left facing flake.  Pull the wild crux and continue up the the anchors.

routes.name: The Punk and the Godfather
routes.FA:: Brian Hibbert, Dave Smart, Nick Pappigeorgio. 1984
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ascend the short finger crack at the base of the White Trash Couloir. Take care climbing around the loose block on the easy ledge above.
routes.Location: Base of White Trash Couloir.

routes.name: Sundog
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy start relative to the top half of the route which contains the crux roof. First bolt is above a ledge, which you can easily reach with a scramble on the right if you want to pre-clip that one as a face climb to the ledge may be a bit nerve-wracking.
routes.Location: Same start as Eclipse, then traverse left. Climber's right of Big Game ledge/access.

routes.name: Weekend Warrior
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A small open book to the left of Lost and Found.

routes.name: The Castle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the crack, taking small gear and fingers.

routes.name: Come and get it
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Pull the small roof then transition to face climbing; sustained difficulties to the top. 75ft when climbed directly from the trail, about 55ft if climbed from the ledge.
routes.Location: 9 ft right of rescue #30

routes.name: Death Block Roulette
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the crack to the right of hybrid. A short roof is encountered along the way. You will reach the far left end of the big game ledge, after which the bolted route taco can be used to reach the top of the cliff and anchors. As the name suggests there are some horrifically large and loose blocks on this route. Climb with caution, don't pull outwards!
routes.Location: Right of hybrid
routes.Protection: Trad gear, bolts after the ledge

routes.name: Elegy For GUT
routes.FA:: FRA Maarten Van Haeren, Justin Dwyer (June 2014)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the arching crack left of Hiromi's Route
routes.Location: Left of Hiromi's Route
routes.Protection: Bolted anchor at top; no rap rings.

routes.name: Hiromi's Route
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Enjoyable long route with solid hands and feet but be careful of some chossy rock.
routes.Location: First sport route you encounter after passing the Falling Tom Gully (heading north) and at the bottom of the small hill.
routes.Protection: Really well bolted with 15 draws needed for the bolts + 2 more draws for the anchors! Easy to skip a bolt here and there if you don’t have enough draws.

routes.name: Hybrid
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Used to be a mixed climb; now it's bolted for your convenience!  The first half of hybrid is a romp up a moderate crack system in a corner.  About halfway up, the holds get thinner and angle steepens.  There are two distinct cruxes separated by a good rest near a small tree.
routes.Location: Starts just left of Rescue Plaque #31

routes.name: Make My Day
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb through the roof before traversing left up the face.

routes.name: Oh Ya It Fits
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb a layback crack a short way up Castle Chimney, move around chockstone's left side.
routes.Location: About 20 meters left of Grunge (Heavy Metal Wall). There is a large chimney (Castle Chimney) on right side of a ledge system.
routes.Protection: std rack, no anchor at top

routes.name: Weekend Warrior
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Location: Located roughly 10 feet right of the typical big game ledge access gully.

routes.name: Where Eagles Dare
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Find in guidebook.

routes.name: Bolero
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on the left or the right of the big block. Layback the flake to the crux finish on the face.
routes.Location: 2m left of "Charleston"

routes.name: Cha Cha
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the line of bolts to the Dance Wall Ledge. Move to the left side of the ledge and pull the roof. Follow the bolts to the top.
routes.Location: Climbers left of Zoomba wall at a low point in the trail.

routes.name: The Charleston
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is the route between Bolero and Zoomba. Slippery move at 2nd bolt leads to crimpy face climbing. Avoid using the crack.

routes.name: Climb Story
routes.FA:: Martin Seidenschmid, Karl Seidenschmid, Mark Jablosky (1988)
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb positive holds to gain the face. Rest on a ledge and then move through the arete onto some crimps. Rest before a final roof. May want to stick clip the first bolt.

routes.name: Foxtrot
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Tricky start leads to excellent climbing trending right.
routes.Location: RESCUE #25

routes.name: Grunge
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The route is located in the Central Zone.  Descend Falling Tom gully and walk north.  The route is marked by a yellow '16b.'  The route starts at a ledge.  This ledge is approximately a meter above the trail proper.  The route follows the obvious book fold with a crack that runs continuously up the fold.  The crack will end at a prominent bulge.  To the left is a ledge with a large tree.  This is your exit.

routes.name: Lest We Forget
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A blocky start eventually leads to a headwall.
routes.Location: Located on the "The Party on Pinnacle" ledge, its the right most bolted line.

routes.name: Mambo
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Located immediately to the right to a grassy gulley. 25fts of easy climbing up from the ground to a ledge. (The start on the ground is to the left of Twist/Two to Tango).From the ledge, there are two hard bolted lines. Mambo is the one on the left.Up from the ledge, several sustained hard moves up an arete using the crack to the left. Then negotiate delicate face climbing to the anchors.
routes.Location: Located immediately to the right to a grassy gulley. 25fts of easy climbing up from the ground to the ledge. (The start on the ground is to the left of Twist/Two to Tango).From the ledge, there are two hard bolted lines. Mambo is the one on the left.

routes.name: Polka
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Right side of the wall beginning on a ledge part way up.

routes.name: Rumba
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb 20 feet to gain the "A" Dance Ledge. Then continue straight up the middle bolted route (tricky high step to reach the small ledge) and then continue through a bulge (crux) to a small roof.
routes.Location: The middle bolted route on the "A" Dance Ledge. Between the Zoomba Wall and Climb Story
routes.Protection: Bolted to a 2 bolt anchor with rings

routes.name: Sian
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts in a crack just left of Shake a Leg. Head right from the ledge higher up, up the stem corner.
routes.Location: Paint #17A

routes.name: Spring Thaw
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb the chimney to the large ledge with a cedar. From here either climb the crack or chimney to the top. Can be broken into 2 pitches.
routes.Location: Chimney between Mambo and Samba
routes.Protection: Standard rack. Large cams can be useful.

routes.name: Stupid Tree
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The only route that isn't polished. On the left side of the Zoomba Wall is a nice crack with a tree growing out of it.
routes.Location: Zoomba Wall

routes.name: Tango
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The official start is on a ledge 25ft off the ground. Difficult start leads to sustained climbing on small holds. Be careful when clipping as leaving the ledge.
routes.Location: Located immediately to the right to a grassy gulley. 25ft of easy climbing up from the ground to the ledge. (The start on the ground is to the left of Twist/Two to Tango).From the ledge, there are two hard bolted lines. Tango is the one on the right.

routes.name: Twist
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Two to Tango. Keep to the right.

routes.name: Two to Tango
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Twist. Keep to the left.

routes.name: Upwardly Mobile
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: First bolt is on right facing rock, which can be clipped from ledge. Traverse and move up. This route shares a short crux section with Climb Story, but then ventures to the left again. Finish on the black rock just behind the cedar at the top.
routes.Location: On a ledge behind some plants.

routes.name: Winter Carnival
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb chimney system on left side of the Party On Pinnacle.
routes.Location: On a pinnacle located about 100m climber's left of Curved Chimney. The pinnacle is atop a steep hill above the base trail.
routes.Protection: std rack. No anchor at top

routes.name: Zoomba
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Left of Foxtrot. Reachy.

routes.name: The 5.10
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ok, we do not know the true name of this route, but did meet the first ascensionist.  We named the route after this person's entertaining, never ending inflation of this route's grade, which has made for hilarious conversation.   The route is approximately 30 meters to the right of marker '19.'  8 bolts.  Wrap at the Fixe anchor.  Very well protected and fun to climb.  The last move before the wrap station is most likely more 5.7 if you go direct.  Going left is easier . . .

routes.name: Bam, Pow, Zowie
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Easy climbing to a hard crux at the chains. One of the bolts mid route is quite rusted.
routes.Location: Sport route that the starts up the gully to the left of the long wait.

routes.name: Crazy Doctor
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Steep face climbing.
routes.Location: Left of the south gully of Ivory Tower.

routes.name: The Long Wait
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Overhanging route on small blocks. Some manteling required, to reach a face above.

routes.name: Zona Rosa
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Run out bolts on the right of the south gully of Ivory Tower.

routes.name: Goose
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route is located on an open rock face to the left of Omen.  Omen is at yellow mark '26a.'  Climb the obvious bolt line (7) to the Fixe anchor.

routes.name: Irreplaceable
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the line of the crack up to the overhang. Stay to the left for almost the entire climb, often using the arete. Big reaches to bolts.
routes.Location: To the left of #27.

routes.name: Unity
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted route that climbs up the blocky face right if the Ivory Tower feature. An interesting crux at the shallow dihedral leads to easier climbing to the top. Flexibility will help if you're a bit stumped.

routes.name: Access
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A short bolted route going up a series of jugs on an overhang to finish at a ledge with a possible connection to Doible feature (trad). No anchors, lower from the last bolt.
routes.Location: 2m right of paint #31, 5m left of rescue #18

routes.name: Aftershock
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The first sport sport (retro-bolted) route on the Geound Zero Ledge. Climb through the top overhang for full value, or dodge right to avoid the difficulties.

routes.name: Double Feature
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb thin corners left of Access to a bolt/pin belay (5.9R). Or climb Access (5.10a sport) or The Hawk’s Nest (5.6 trad - bottom of Mephisto) to the same belay. The second pitch climbs the 5.8 crack on the right side of the ledge to the Mephisto anchors.

routes.name: Ground Zero
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start by climbing 7 meters up the face to a ledge below a huge corner.  Climb the crack system to a stance beneath an imposing overhanging offwidth/chimney.  Place a large cam and commit; you won't get any more gear until established above the roof.  Once you have surmounted the roof, climb easier wide cracks in the corner to anchors just below the cliff top.  A 60m rope will reach the ground.
routes.Location: The climb starts at Rescue plaque #19
routes.Protection: Trad gear with emphasis on larger pieces.  Doubles of 2-4 camalots would probably be useful.  There are bolted anchors.

routes.name: Mephisto
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Mixed route. From the ground, trad climb 5.6 terrain to the first bolt. Not recommended due to fall potential. Possible to do on TR if set up after climbing Double Feature.
routes.Location: Start left of paint #31
routes.Protection: Mixed route, bolted at too 1/3 of route.

routes.name: The Cat's Tail
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts in the bottom of the short chasm at mark 35a. Follows the finger crack through the corner and up to a hand crack. Follow the layback section then up through the flakes before working left. Up through the roof to bolted top anchors. The original route ending went left across the face from here, around the corner to an obvious finish. The top anchors were placed to reduce stress on the cedars.
routes.Protection: The route eats pro.  Small nuts to #3 camalot.

routes.name: The Cat's Whisker
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: up a wide blocky crack ending on a vegetated ledge.
routes.Location: Large blocky crack on the Cat's Tail Wall

routes.name: Dogleg
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start by scrambling up large cracks that form a huge corner to the right of Swan's Song.  Be sure to bring your spider wand (a.k.a. a large stick).  When you reach the huge roof make a hard left, hand traversing on the slimy crack (beware of falling on cams placed in this fissure). At the end of the traverse, head straight up on clean cracks.  Alternatively, if the spiders (or the cracks) look too menacing, you can easily escape onto the upper portion of Swan Song.  Either way, finish at the Swan Song anchors.
routes.Location: The huge corner on the right side of the Cat's Tail area, 20 meters right of Rescue #16.
routes.Protection: Single rack to 4 inches. Nuts are useful.  Long slings are indespensable.

routes.name: The Graduate
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: One of Nemo's best climbs at the grade. This is the bolted line a couple of meters right of Cat's Tail. Climb the face right of the arête, and the face above, until you can get a closer look at the large roof. Take a deep breath. Exit the roof using the obvious crack. Fight the pump to the top.
routes.Location: 43.425825, -79.884084In between 5.9 Cat's Tail and 5.10d Peanut Butter BrothersThe route start where the red # 16 is
routes.Protection: 12 Bolts + AnchorsPermadraw under the roof and fixed anchor carabineers to facilitate cleaning added August 2024

routes.name: Knees and Elbows
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb chimney gully left of Cat's Tail. Option to climb through a hidden cave system.
routes.Location: Right of Ray's Ceiling

routes.name: O. The Cat's Tail | 1040
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Scramble to the top of a pillar near the face. Make the first clip by reaching across the gap from here. There are two variations at the third clip. Heading left is easier than straight up (5.12). Don't fall going for the second bolt.

routes.name: Ray's Ceiling
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb wide crack into a chimney, and rappel off anchors for Female Belay.
routes.Location: Right of Old Route.

routes.name: Route 66
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start in the chimney behind the pillar. Shares the first few draws with Route 69, but takes the right trending line through steeper terrain. Some big moves lead to more techy face climbing.
routes.Location: To the left of Female Belay Slave behind the pillar.

routes.name: Route 77
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jul 3, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Bolted by Karl in his 77th year, a nice (5.10b) branching off of Route 66 (5.10d). Start by scrambling up easy 3rd class terrain between the pillar and the wall to a bolt. Climb up Route 66 until you reach the third or fourth bolt and start traversing out left (a couple of long draws will help with rope drag). Work your way out of the traverse and climb through the right side of the roof. Finish on easier terrain to the anchors.
routes.Protection: 9 bolts. Don't fall while the pillar is behind you.
routes.Location: This route starts just to the left of a prominent 30 foot tall pillar, about 50 meters left of the Graduate.

routes.name: Second Agreement
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start just to the right of The Big F.  Ascend the crack that tends to the right side of the Big F's roof.  Continue up and right over seemingly blank terrain to the Fixe Anchors.

routes.name: Stuck in the Middle
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start as if doing the Big F. At the overhanging pillar, step left and ascend the offwidth crack to the ledge with a tree. Pro to #4. From this point you can ascend to the cliff top. Alternatively, you can traverse right over easy terrain to the Fixe anchors atop Second Agreement.

routes.name: Swan Song
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Some tricky moves up to a ledge below a small overhang. Pull the overhang and continue on good holds and through a crimpy crux to the top.

routes.name: Carnal Sin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb decent rock to the first large ledge. Head up and left, pulling on a couple of overhanging blocks to reach the first crack, which can be protected with a BD #4; this crack varies in size but is generally BD #3 and #4. Pull the moves through the crack and get situated beneath the final overhanging and steep hand crack.The hand crack provides excellent jams in the BD #2 range. Pull the roof and fight the pump as the upper crack narrows to BD #1. Finish on the anchors (bolts with rap rings).The upper roof crack is an incredible 4-star hand crack.
routes.Location: Look for the roof with a hand crack to the left of the birch tree on the ledge. See the beta photo.

routes.name: High Society
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Gear at the bottom, bolts up top, anchor. Starts on easy terrain with plenty of pro and gets harder beginning at the slanted crack under the roof. Exit right to a small ledge and the first bolt. Continue up to the anchors on bolts. Bring an alpine draw for your last piece under the roof, and perhaps a somewhat longer sport draw to minimize rope drag at one bolt
routes.Location: At the right side of Morphine Wall.
routes.Protection: Standard rack; a 0.5 cam will protect the roof, along with an alpine (120cm) draw. Sport draws for the upper section, though a single longer one partway up will prevent the rope from running over a small edge.

routes.name: Long Reach
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: At the left end of Morphine Ledge Wall is  is chute.  This prominent line of weakness starts through broken terrain and tends in a generally left direction over broken terrain.  The lower section of the route can be a bit spooky; test the holds before using them.  However, half way up, the route gives way to solid terrain.  You will top out at a ledge just below the cliff top.  Belay here.  Rappelling from where you belayed your partner up is problematic, as it is a less than optimal wrap location.  To depart the route are two options: 1) ascend the obvious line of weakness to the cliff top; 2) down climb to the ledge one meter below where you belayed and rappel at the wrap station.

routes.name: Reunion
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: At Morphine are two prominent roofs.  Bisecting these two roofs is a book fold.  At the base is a yellow '39.'  This is your route.  Ascend the face.  Pull the crux move and continue up to the book fold and step left to the ledge with the large cedar tree.  From here you wrap.  An alternative is to continue up the roof through the crack, which is 5.10.  This is a very well protected route.  It is very much a Seneca style 5.7, so expect technical climbing and committing moves.

routes.name: Reunion Direct
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Begin on Reunion, climbing up loose rock to a large ledge. Make your way up the offwidth (protects well with a BD #4) between the two roofs of Sister Morphine and Carnal Sin. Trend up and climbers right beneath the first large roof (protects well with a BD #5), then traverse left under the roof to another large ledge. From here, head straight up to the steep hand crack at the top of the cliff.The route's crux is the overhanging hand crack at the top of the cliff, which is well protected by multiple BD #3s. Pull the small roof and climb the rest of the crack to the anchors (bolts with rap rings).Reunion Direct is rated 5.9 on rakkup, but I thought that attempting the crux roof moves onsight is at least 5.10-.A 60-meter rope barely reaches the ground from the anchors at the top.
routes.Location: Located on the Morphine Ledge.

routes.name: Sister Morphine
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A beautiful and inspiring route. The roof is intimidating but the difficult section is short. Scramble up pitch 1 (5.7) to the Morphine Ledge. Climb a finger crack to the roof with a fixed nut in the corner. A 10' hand crack in the roof gives way to a vertical off-width before a short finish to the anchor
routes.Location: The obvious giant roof with the hand crack
routes.Protection: Cams from .4-3", fixed nut in corner of roof at the top of the finger crack. Rings at the anchor

routes.name: Trajection
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb decent rock to the first large ledge beneath the flakes and the right of the birch tree. Ascend up and right on good gear, then to the left on the large flakes. Layback and jam the flakes trending left until you are in line with the Carnal Sin anchors. Traverse left until you are slightly above the Carnal Sin anchors. I thought the physical crux of the route was the last of the large flakes, while the mental crux was the leftward traverse after finishing the flakes.There are a couple of options to finish the route. Regardless of your finish, protect the traverse after the flakes for your follower.Downclimb a few moves and end on the Carnal Sin anchors. If you choose this option, note that your follower will be above the anchors and on lead at some point.Continue climbing up and left from the top of the Carnal Sin anchors and end on a tree slung with a couple of rap rings.
routes.Location: Look for the large flakes to the right of Carnal Sin (see beta photo).

routes.name: The Camel
routes.FA:: Helmut Microys, Michael Rosenberger, Francois Garneau (1963/1964)
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: In the middle of the Camel Area is a left facing series of features (flakes and small corner up a very solid line of rock. Climb around the small overhanging features. Ascend to a ledge. Climbing the slightly overhanging line to the wrap station (three bolts).Three bolts (glue in) plus rings.

routes.name: Double Lead
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: To the left of the prominent arch that constitutes Harlequin is a series of large and blocky right facing features. It looks downright ugly, but looks deceive. Climb through these left facing features, tending left. As you approach the cliff top, traverse right to the Fixe anchors. This is a good introductory trad route that offers good practice in rope management over relatively well protected terrain. Make sure you have a follower to clean, retrieving your last piece before the traverse to anchors is very difficult on rappel

routes.name: Grim Thin Flake
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: A retro-bolted old-school trad route. The crux is right at the beginning, protected by two well placed bolts. Angle slight left to obvious flake. Follow to anchors.
routes.Location: About 10m right of Camel, where a thin, steep crack is protected with two bolts.

routes.name: Harlequin
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: In the Camel area there is a prominent buttress of which has two prominent roofs. The start of the route ascends the slab just left of the lower roof. Climb up and right towards the lower roof. Stay left of lower roof and aim for the left facing corner that leads up to the higher roof. Climb left around the higher roof. Traverse left under the cedar. Climb up onto the ledge where the wrap station resides. This is a technical, sustained 5.7.

routes.name: Jay
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts at the broken up section of wall 6m right of Camel. Climb this to the crack above which leads to the same ledge as Camel. To finish continue up and left (5.3) and build an anchor from the exposed rock, or take the Camel crack (5.7) to the Camel  anchors.

routes.name: Old Breed
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Originally climbed on gear, it was bolted in 2012.

routes.name: Positron
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This route resides on the north end of the Camel Wall. It is just left of J-Bean, which is marked by a yellow, '47.' At the start is a slight bulge. This connects to a very clean, left arching flake. This flake terminates at a roof. The original version of this route (as I was told) continued right of this roof, through easier climbing. However, a more fun (and 5.9) variation is to ascend the crack in the roof and continue up the face past two bolts to the fixed anchor. Excellent route!

routes.name: Reg's Route
routes.Shared By::
Randy Von Zee
            on Jun 16, 2018

                             · Updates
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on bolts left of double lead, but then crosses over it.

routes.name: Thunder Road
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Immediately to the left of Camel is a shallow, left facing flake (quite solid) whose crack gradually enlarges as it moves up the face. Ascend this flake system to a bulge (crack will continue up and right), which is to the left. Pull the bulge, which has no natural protection, but has a bolt. Atop the bulge a crack emerges and ascends up a shallow, right facing corner. This crack eats pro. Climb this to a resting stance. Gain the ledge to the wrap station. Note: someone denigrated this route by bolting it in its entirety. The route is truly a mixed route, with the only required bolt being the one at the bulge.
routes.Protection: Mixed.  Please be an honorable climber: use pro.

routes.name: Bei Tageslicht
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start on flakes and move up over the crux buldge using a hidden hold to an overhang.
routes.Location: The bolted route left of Ein Kleines Nacht Klettern.

routes.name: Big Sky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb flakes and overhangs to the top.

routes.name: Butterfingers
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Starts at marker #49. Ascend the varied terrain up to a crack that sits in a right facing corner. Continue up the incipient crack to the roof. Pull the roof. Fixed wrap station. An alternate 5.6 variation: same start. Ascend the arching left facing corner (is immediately to the right of the right facing corner. The arching crack and right facing corner converge 1/2 way up the route. Traverse left at the incipient crack. Gain the ledge to the left. Ascend to the 2nd ledge and traverse right to the wrap station. Both variations are quite good.

routes.name: Ein Kleines Nacht Klettern
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: This is a short, obvious crack where The Big Gulp begins and often has plenty of climbers practicing on it.
routes.Location: Beside easy way down.
routes.Protection: Gear required. Anchors at the top.

routes.name: Four No More
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Start at Palm Sunday but climb up and left of the shallow cave to reach some face climbing.
routes.Location: Paint #50

routes.name: The Head Mucky Mucky
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb up blocks to a big ledge then continue up the easy crack. Easy, blocky climbing.
routes.Location: Right beside the Penguin Hatch gully.

routes.name: Judy's on the Drug Squad
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Ascend the blocky face to a right facing flake that grows into a corner.  At the corner's terminus, ascend up and right through the bulge to easier climbing.
routes.Location: The route is at the right-most end of the Big Gulp area.  There is nearly always a party camped here, so you cannot miss it.
routes.Protection: This was once a trad route, but has recently been re-bolted with anchors.

routes.name: Madames Deconvience
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Clip the bolt under the roof and step left then head up. Use a long runner on the first bolt.

routes.name: Palm Sunday
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: The route is located on a semi-independent formation to the right of Butterfingers. This formation is slightly uphill of all surrounding cliff. Ascend and move right to a shallow, left leaning ramp, which is located on the middle of the face. Continue up the steep terrain to the rap station.

routes.name: Peanut Butter Punch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Unknown route between Bei Tageslicht and Trauma Belay.   A bit dirty with some loose rock and crux up high.

routes.name: The Penguin Hatch
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: An awkward move is needed to get into the crevice, which traverses behind the cliff to the exit up near "Wormhole".
routes.Location: About 150m climbers left of the "easy way down" is the entrance to a long crevice cave called Penguin Hatch.
routes.Protection: not worth placing protection either solo it or don't climb it

routes.name: Proton
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Climb solid flakes right of Butterfingers ending at the top of Butterfingers.
routes.Location: Start on Butterfingers

routes.name: Red Line Fever
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Brilliant sustained route. Climb the left edge before stepping right into the crack and harder climbing above.

routes.name: Sidewinder
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Shared start with Judy's. Head left after the third bolt.

routes.name: Trauma Belay
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Retro-bolted route going up blocks to short slab up top.
routes.Location: Left of Bei Tageslicht

routes.name: Wasp In Space
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Follow the bolts to the anchor above.
routes.Location: Left of Madames Deconvenience.

routes.name: Wormhole
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Chimney climb, squeeze under the stuck boulder.
routes.Location: Left of Red Line Fever.

routes.name: Yellow Streak
routes.Admins:: Dave Rone,    Scott Graham Page Updates (admin only)
routes.Description: Choose your own adventure up through the gully.
routes.Location: Start on a ledge just a little further up from Four No More.
